messages.content: ugh ok so patreon hit 127 patrons this month which is... 840 a month now? math is hard when you're stressed but that feels right
That's solid growth! $840/month gives you a nice foundation. What's got you stressed about it?

it's not the patreon exactly, more like... i keep looking at this arturia minifreak, it's 550 bucks and would be perfect for the ambient stuff i'm working on but then my brain goes straight to quarterly taxes
Ah, the classic gear vs taxes dilemma. When's your Q3 payment due?

estimated payment is due this month and i haven't even calculated it yet which is... not great. but like the minifreak has these morphing wavetables that would be insane for the textural layers i'm hearing in my head
Maybe tackle the tax estimate first so you know exactly what you're working with? Then you can make the gear decision with a clearer picture.
yeah you're right, it's just hard when inspiration hits and you know exactly what sound you need but the responsible adult part of your brain is like 'hey remember the irs exists'
The IRS definitely doesn't care about your creative flow, unfortunately. But getting the taxes sorted might actually free up mental space for the music work.
true, and honestly the patreon growth has been encouraging... maybe by next quarter i'll be in a better spot to justify the gear splurge without the guilt spiral
Exactly - that steady monthly income is building something sustainable. The gear will still be there when your cash flow feels more comfortable.

summary: Sam discusses Patreon reaching 127 patrons ($840/month) while wrestling with wanting to buy an Arturia MiniFreak synthesizer versus handling overdue quarterly tax payments. The conversation drifts between creative gear desires and financial responsibilities.
